title: The Sea-Child by Eliza Cook — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
HE crawls to the cliff and plays on a brink
Where every eye but his own would shrink;
No music he hears but the billow’s noise,
And shells and weeds are his only toys.
No lullaby can the mother find
To sing him to rest like the moaning wind;
And the louder it wails and the fiercer it sweeps,
The deeper he breathes and the sounder he sleeps.
meaning: "The Sea-Child" by Eliza Cook is a sad and love and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 35 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "HE crawls to the cliff and plays on a brink Where every eye but his own would shrink;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Eliza Cook's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
